title: TreeBench-861: A Benchmark for Hierarchy-Sensitive Retrieval over Structured Regulatory Corpora
description: TreeBench is a benchmark of 861 questions designed to expose structural evidence failures in retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) over hierarchically structured corpora. It targets structural confounder pairs — nodes that are semantically similar but structurally incompatible — across 5 regulatory domains and 10 structural failure types derived from the U.S. Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (eCFR). The best non-oracle method achieves 76% answer accuracy but only 45% required-node recall, producing correct-looking answers while missing the structural evidence that authorizes them.
notes: The dataset is also available on HuggingFace at sahilsoni2409/TreeBench. Code is released under MIT; data under CC-BY-4.0.
